response:
*KEEP THE AREA CLEAN*  
BRUSH GENTLY AND THOROUGHLY TWICE A DAY  
RINSE WITH WATER AFTER EVERY MEAL  
*FLOSS CAREFULLY*  
FLOSE AROUND THE AFFECTED TOOTH  
REMOVE FOOD PARTICLES GENTLY  
*AVOID CERTAIN FOODS*  
STAY AWAY FROM HARD OR STICKY FOODS  
REDUCE PAIN FROM EXTREME TEMPERATURES  
*TEMPORARY FILLING MATERIALS*  
USE OVER THE COUNTERTOOTH MATERIALS  
PROTECT THE TOOTH UNTIL A DENTIST IS AVAILABLE  
*MONITOR FOR CHANGES*  
KEEP AN EYE ON THE TOOTH FOR SIGNS OF INFECTION  
INCREASED PAIN OR SENSITIVITY MAY INDICATE A PROBLEM  

* * *  

*SAFETY FIRST*  
USE SALINE WATER RINSE TO REDUCE INFLAMMATION  
BACTERIA ARE PREVENTED BY GENTLE RINSING  
REMEDY PAIN WITH OTC MEDICATIONS AS DIRECTED  
*RELIABLE CARE AVAILABLE*  
DENTAL SCHOOLS OR EXPATRIATE COMMUNITIES CAN PROVIDE HELP  
LOCATION MATTERS WHEN SEEKING DENTAL CARE  
*EXTENDED STAYS REQUIRE PROACTIVE ACTION*  
DELAYING TREATMENT MAY LEAD TO MORE SEVERE ISSUES
